subject: FW: Representative Volatility Trades
body:
From: Duncan Yin [mailto:dyin@hdam.com] 
Sent: Thursday, February 14, 2008 12:08 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Representative Volatility Trades

 

Jeffrey,

 

Bruce told me that you wanted to see examples of specific volatility
trades and how they performed upon maturity.  Please find attached a
three-page memo which describes two representative trades, the first put
on in a "normal" environment before volatility spiked last July and the
second after volatility was already high.

 

Speaking very generally, trades put on which mature in a "normal" market
with moderate volatility aim to earn 200% to 300% of the capital at
risk.  Trades put on in a market of high volatility such as we are
currently experiencing aim to make about 100% of the capital at risk.
By risking only 10% of our total capital base, we aim to generate 20% to
30% returns on the entire portfolio during typical flat or rising
markets and 10% returns in falling markets.  Both historical and current
market pricing data are consistent with these targets.

 

I look forward to meeting you today at 3:00 PM and hearing your comments
on this material.  Thank you again for your interest.

 

Regards,

Duncan A. Yin

 

Work:  (203) 569-4524

Cell:  (203) 856-8148

 

------------------------------------------------------------------------
------------------------------------------------------

This correspondence is intended for the addressee only. The information
in this correspondence has been obtained from sources which Harbor Drive
Asset Management LLC ("HDAM") believes to be reliable. However, HDAM
does not guarantee its accuracy, and such information may be incomplete
or condensed. Opinions in this correspondence represent HDAM's position
as of this date and are subject to change without notice. This
correspondence is for informational purposes only and is not intended as
an offer or solicitation in regard to the purchase or sale of any
security. HDAM and/or its officers and employees may from time to time
acquire, hold, or sell a position in the securities mentioned herein.
Upon request, HDAM will furnish specific information in this regard. If
HDAM is involved in the purchase or sale of any security, HDAM may act
as principal for its own account or as agent for the buyer and/or the
seller.
body_html:
From: Duncan Yin [mailto:dyin@hdam.com]
Sent: Thursday, February 14, 2008 12:08 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Representative Volatility Trades

 

Jeffrey,

 

Bruce told me that you wanted to see examples of specific volatility trades and how they performed upon maturity.  Please find attached a three-page memo which describes two representative trades, the first put on in a “normal” environment before volatility spiked last July and the second after volatility was already high.

 

Speaking very generally, trades put on which mature in a “normal” market with moderate volatility aim to earn 200% to 300% of the capital at risk.  Trades put on in a market of high volatility such as we are currently experiencing aim to make about 100% of the capital at risk.  By risking only 10% of our total capital base, we aim to generate 20% to 30% returns on the entire portfolio during typical flat or rising markets and 10% returns in falling markets.  Both historical and current market pricing data are consistent with these targets.

 

I look forward to meeting you today at 3:00 PM and hearing your comments on this material.  Thank you again for your interest.

 

Regards,

Duncan A. Yin

 

Work:  (203) 569-4524

Cell:  (203) 856-8148

 

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This correspondence is intended for the addressee only. The information in this correspondence has been obtained from sources which Harbor Drive Asset Management LLC ("HDAM") believes to be reliable. However, HDAM does not guarantee its accuracy, and such information may be incomplete or condensed. Opinions in this correspondence represent HDAM’s position as of this date and are subject to change without notice. This correspondence is for informational purposes only and is not intended as an offer or solicitation in regard to the purchase or sale of any security. HDAM and/or its officers and employees may from time to time acquire, hold, or sell a position in the securities mentioned herein. Upon request, HDAM will furnish specific information in this regard. If HDAM is involved in the purchase or sale of any security, HDAM may act as principal for its own account or as agent for the buyer and/or the seller.
